# n attribute: metarule only matches targets that exist on disk.
# With n, the %.o metarule skips bar.o (doesn't exist) even though bar.c does.
# Without n, the metarule matches bar.o regardless.

# With n: foo.o exists → metarule matches → edge in graph.
#         bar.o doesn't exist → metarule skips → no edge.
mk -dot -f mkfile.n
stdout '"foo.o" -> "foo.c"'
! stdout '"bar.o" -> "bar.c"'

# Without n: both targets matched → both edges in graph.
mk -dot -f mkfile.non
stdout '"foo.o" -> "foo.c"'
stdout '"bar.o" -> "bar.c"'

-- mkfile.n --
%.o:n: %.c
	compile $stem.c
all:V: foo.o bar.o
	echo done

-- mkfile.non --
%.o: %.c
	compile $stem.c
all:V: foo.o bar.o
	echo done

-- foo.o --
old object
-- foo.c --
source
-- bar.c --
source
